Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The 185-Microsecond Type Hint 53 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 262 upvotes, 50 comments
  3. The Cathode Ray Tube site 22 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. "That Shape Had None" – A Horror of Substrate Independence (Short Fiction) 84 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. Inside the M4 Apple Neural Engine, Part 1: Reverse Engineering 288 upvotes, 75 comments
  6. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 255 upvotes, 72 comments
  7. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 2100 upvotes, 752 comments
  8. Seed of Might Color Correction Process (2023) [pdf] 76 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. Show HN: Visual Lambda Calculus – a thesis project (2008) revived for the web 27 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. RCade: Building a Community Arcade Cabinet 61 upvotes, 14 comments
  11. How to Build Your Own Quantum Computer 54 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Show HN: Govbase – Follow a bill from source text to news bias to social posts 169 upvotes, 72 comments
  13. Ars Technica Fires Reporter After AI Controversy Involving Fabricated Quotes 44 upvotes, 14 comments
  14. British Columbia to end time changes, adopt year-round daylight time 540 upvotes, 282 comments
  15. Closure of the Weatheradio Service in Canada 108 upvotes, 48 comments
  16. Against Query Based Compilers 42 upvotes, 15 comments
  17. The workers behind Meta’s smart glasses can see everything 753 upvotes, 439 comments
  18. LFortran compiles fpm 56 upvotes, 23 comments
  19. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 69 upvotes, 30 comments
  20. Simple Screw Counter 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Programmable Cryptography 56 upvotes, 31 comments
  22. Welcome (back) to Macintosh 276 upvotes, 201 comments
  23. Parallel coding agents with tmux and Markdown specs 133 upvotes, 107 comments
  24. Elevated Errors in Claude.ai 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. Ask HN: Who is hiring? (March 2026) 184 upvotes, 233 comments
  26. iPhone 17e 223 upvotes, 305 comments
  27. New iPad Air, powered by M4 345 upvotes, 552 comments
  28. Show HN: Giggles – A batteries-included React framework for TUIs 6 upvotes, 6 comments
  29. Ask HN: Who wants to be hired? (March 2026) 79 upvotes, 196 comments
  30. Reflex (YC W23) Is Hiring Software Engineers – Python 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 12:30pm, 3 March 2026. Source here.
